what: `tools/wiring_checker.py` (Phase 1) is a standalone Python CLI that performs static call-graph / dead-code analysis on changed source files using the stdlib `ast` module. For each file it builds a call graph (mapping function/method definitions to their call sites), identifies functions/methods never called from any real (non-test) execution path, and for each dead-code finding emits a candidate WIRING coverage item as JSON to stdout (`type: "WIRING"` + the unreachable symbol name). It implements Requirement 8 (Wiring Verification, baseline block B07, PRD refs REQ-EXEC-010..012) — the "components that compile but are never connected" detector — at design.md:135 described as "Static call-grap…

open_gaps.gap: Criterion 8.1's second clause is unfulfilled: wiring_checker.py emits a WIRING candidate to STDOUT only; no component ingests that candidate into feature_list.json. The requirement says 'SHALL represent each wiring obligation as a WIRING coverage item in feature_list.json', but the data path stops at the post-hook's next-turn feedback text. No task creates the WIRING item from the candidate.
open_gaps.fix: Add a task (or extend task 19.1) defining the ingestion path: e.g., the initializer/implementer agent, on receiving the WIRING candidate as next-turn feedback, MUST add a corresponding type:'WIRING' CoverageItem to feature_list.json via the PreToolUse status-guard-permitted creation path; OR have wiring_checker.py (or the post-hook) write the item directly under the artifact-guard exception. Specify which actor writes it and how the PreToolUse artifact/status guards (design.md:214-215) admit a n…

open_gaps.gap: Criterion 8.2 (Verifier SHALL mark the WIRING item failed on an unreachable handler/route/job/callback) has NO concrete mechanism. The Verifier definition (verifier.md key-behaviors, design.md:255-266) never reads wiring_checker.py output, never mentions WIRING items, and never performs an unproven→failed transition for wiring. There is no task connecting the checker's unreachable-symbol finding to a Verifier-authored failed transition.
open_gaps.fix: Add a verifier.md key-behavior + a task: 'On each slice, the Verifier consumes the wiring_checker.py findings (or re-runs it) and, for any WIRING item whose symbol is reported unreachable, performs the unproven→failed transition (the PreToolUse status guard already permits unproven→failed, design.md:215).' Cite REQ-EXEC-011 and add a unit/property test asserting unreachable→failed.

open_gaps.gap: No Z3 CHECK and no correctness Property pins Requirement 8 / wiring_checker.py. Every other major gate carries a CHECK or a Property; wiring is logically un-pinned. The integration claim 'looks done cannot pass as is wired' rests solely on two example-based tests (19.2, 26.1).
open_gaps.fix: Either (a) add a Property (e.g., 'Property 31: Wiring/Dead-Code Detection — for any file with a defined-but-never-called non-test symbol, wiring_checker.py emits exactly one WIRING candidate naming that symbol; for any reachable handler, zero candidates') with a Hypothesis test, OR (b) explicitly document in design.md that wiring detection is a runtime/example-tested property with no Z3 model (as was done for the audit-log chain at design.md:1110). Today the absence is silent.

open_gaps.gap: Design thinness: wiring_checker.py gets exactly ONE row in the component inventory (design.md:135) and ZERO narrative/interface/algorithm description in the Components-and-Interfaces body, the data-flow diagram, or any B07/Requirement-8 design section. There is no design discussion of how 'real execution path' is defined for AST reachability, how cross-file/cross-module calls are handled (the task says 'build a call graph for each file' — i.e., per-file, which cannot see callers in other files), or how entry points are seeded. A per-file AST call graph structurally CANNOT distinguish 'defined-but-never-called-in-this-file' from 'called-from-another-file', so it will false-positive on every p…
open_gaps.fix: Add a design subsection for B07 specifying: entry-point seeding (CLI mains, route decorators, registered callbacks, test files excluded), whether reachability is whole-repo or per-file (and if per-file, how exported symbols are exempted to avoid false positives), and the precise definition of 'real execution path'. Reconcile the per-file scope in tasks.md:327 against the cross-file false-positive risk; likely the analysis must be repo-wide, not per-file.

open_gaps.gap: Changed-files input contract is asymmetric/underspecified between caller and tool. post_tool_use_hook.py is 'Triggered on Write/Edit/MultiEdit completion' (tasks.md:310) but the task never states how it derives the changed-file list it passes to wiring_checker.py (hook stdin JSON tool_input? git diff?). wiring_checker.py 'Accept a list of changed files as CLI args' (tasks.md:326) but the handoff format (one path per arg vs newline list) is unspecified.
open_gaps.fix: Specify in task 18.1 how the post-hook extracts the edited file path(s) from the PostToolUse hook payload (the tool_input.file_path field) and passes them as argv to wiring_checker.py; and state in task 19.1 the exact argv contract (e.g., one file path per positional arg).

open_gaps.gap: Overlap/role ambiguity with the Semgrep wiring rules (task 24.1) and orphan_detector.py (task 20) is never resolved. wiring_dead_code.yml targets decorator-based routes/handlers and unwired callbacks (tasks.md:385-386) — exactly the route/handler/callback obligations in criterion 8.2 and the glossary — while wiring_checker.py's AST approach only finds defined-but-never-called functions (tasks.md:328). It is unclear which tool authoritatively detects route/handler/callback wiring, whether both feed the same WIRING items, and whether they can double-report.
open_gaps.fix: Add a one-line division-of-labor note: e.g., 'wiring_checker.py (AST) covers plain dead functions; wiring_dead_code.yml (Semgrep) covers decorator-routed handlers and registered-but-uninvoked callbacks; both emit type:WIRING candidates de-duplicated by symbol before ingestion.' Cross-reference tasks 19, 24, and the criterion-8.2 handler/route/callback list.

open_gaps.gap: wiring_checker.py output is invisible to observability and audit. Unlike the gate hooks (which call audit_log.append, design.md:209-218) and the OTel span emitters, the wiring check produces no span and no audit entry, so 'we can prove the wiring check ran/what it found' is not part of the tamper-evident record — despite wiring being a completeness pillar (pain-point cat 1/4).
open_gaps.fix: Decide and document whether wiring findings should be recorded: minimally have post_tool_use_hook.py emit an OTel PostToolUse gate-decision event including the wiring-check summary (it is already in scope for task 36.1 'extend all six hooks to emit OTel gate-decision events'), and note that the PostToolUse path is non-gating so it is informational, not audit-chained.

critic_findings.detail: The build task for THIS component, task 19.1, carries `_Requirements: 8.1, 8.2_` (tasks.md:331) — i.e. it CLAIMS to deliver criterion 8.2 (the Verifier marks the WIRING item `failed` on an unreachable handler/route/job/callback). But the task BODY (tasks.md:326-330) only builds AST analysis + a stdout WIRING candidate and explicitly exits 0; it does NOT perform or wire any Verifier `unproven→failed` transition. The draft's 8.2 ears_story (status 'gap') correctly says 'No task connects the checker's finding to a Verifier failed transition,' but it fails to flag the sharper defect: task 19.1's own traceability line FALSELY claims 8.2 coverage that its body does not deliver. This is an internal…
critic_findings.cit: tasks.md:331 (task 19.1 _Requirements: 8.1, 8.2_) vs tasks.md:326-330 (body); tasks.md:388, tasks.md:410; requirements.md:173 (criterion 8.2)

critic_findings.detail: The draft's properties_and_checks asserts: 'The ONLY verification artifact bearing on this component: task 19.2 unit tests (tasks.md:333-336) and task 26.1 Phase-1 integration test (tasks.md:404-405).' This OMITS task 42.1 — the final PBT-suite consolidation/gate-registration task — whose `_Requirements:` line explicitly includes 8.1 (tasks.md:718). Task 42.1 is a third verification artifact traced to this component's primary criterion (8.1) and is also the run-locally-and-fix gate. The draft's enumeration of verification artifacts is therefore incomplete and understates the (nominal) coverage that the spec assigns to wiring_checker.py.
critic_findings.cit: tasks.md:718 (task 42.1 _Requirements: ... 8.1 ...); draft properties_and_checks 3rd bullet ('ONLY verification artifact ... 19.2 ... 26.1')

critic_findings.detail: The draft never cites Property 1 (Coverage Item Schema Invariant), which directly governs the WIRING items this checker produces. Property 1 (design.md:882-886, 'Validates: Requirements 5.1, 5.4') states every item shall have 'a `type` from {functional, NFR, WIRING}, at least one acceptance criterion, and a `status` defaulting to `unproven`.' The checker emits `type:"WIRING"` candidates that must conform to exactly this invariant once ingested. The draft's properties_and_checks flatly claims 'none [of the 30 Properties] covers call-graph/dead-code/wiring' and its 5.4 ears_story (status 'partial') discusses the WIRING enum membership without naming Property 1. Property 1 is the schema-invaria…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:882-886 (Property 1, Validates 5.1/5.4); tasks.md:33-34 (Property 1 test, Validates 5.1/5.4); draft properties_and_checks 1st bullet & 5.4 ears_story

critic_findings.detail: There is no ears_story for criterion 13.2 (REQ-STEER-002), even though wiring_checker.py runs entirely inside the path that 13.2 governs. Criterion 13.2 (requirements.md:251) mandates 'THE PostToolUse_Hook SHALL be treated as a next-turn forcing function that cannot undo an already-executed action.' The Hook Wiring Table (design.md:216) maps the PostToolUse row — which runs the wiring check — to REQ-VERIFY-004 AND REQ-STEER-002, and tasks 18.1 (tasks.md:316: `_Requirements: 9.4, 13.2_`) and 26.1 (tasks.md:410: includes 13.2) both trace the wiring-check path to 13.2. The draft's own 'when' narrative depends on this 'non-blocking, cannot-undo, next-turn-feedback' semantics, yet the ears_storie…
critic_findings.cit: requirements.md:251 (criterion 13.2); design.md:216 (PostToolUse row → REQ-STEER-002); tasks.md:316, tasks.md:410

critic_findings.detail: The draft's 8.3 ears_story and the glossary/CoverageItem-type stories do not cite the schema-level encoding of the WIRING→integration-test obligation. design.md:334 documents the `type` enum with 'WIRING items require integration-test evidence,' which is the data-model anchor of criterion 8.3 (requirements.md:174) and directly constrains items of the type this checker produces. The draft cites criterion 8.3 only via task 47 (tasks.md:959) and treats 8.3 as a 'generic Evidence_Record gate ... does not involve wiring_checker.py.' That is defensible, but omitting the design.md:334 schema note understates how tightly the WIRING type the checker emits is bound to the integration-test evidence rul…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:334 ('WIRING items require integration-test evidence'); requirements.md:174 (criterion 8.3); draft 8.3 ears_story

critic_findings.detail: The draft's behaviors and config_settings sections do not record the caller-side `MultiEdit` coupling completeness. The PostToolUse matcher is `Write|Edit|MultiEdit` (design.md:178, settings.json) and the PostToolUse trigger row fires on 'Write, Edit, MultiEdit completion' (design.md:216), so the wiring check is invoked after MultiEdit edits too. The draft's config_settings 'PostToolUse matcher' entry lists the default `Write|Edit|MultiEdit` correctly, but its changed-files-input-contract gap (open_gaps) discusses only Write/Edit and omits that MultiEdit can mutate multiple files in one call — sharpening the underspecified 'how does the post-hook derive the changed-file list' gap (a single M…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:178, design.md:216 (matcher/trigger Write|Edit|MultiEdit); tasks.md:310 (post-hook trigger); draft open_gaps 'Changed-files input contract'

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL run static call-graph and dead-code analysis on changed files and SHALL represent each wiring obligation as a WIRING coverage item in feature_list.json.
ears_needing_work.how: wiring_checker.py performs the AST call-graph + dead-code analysis (tasks.md:327-328) and emits a WIRING coverage-item *candidate* as JSON to stdout (tasks.md:329). The first clause (analysis) is addressed; the second clause ('represent each wiring obligation as a WIRING coverage item in feature_lis…

ears_needing_work.criterion: IF a handler, route, job, or callback exists but is unreachable from any real execution path, THEN THE Verifier SHALL mark the corresponding WIRING item failed.
ears_needing_work.how: No concrete mechanism. wiring_checker.py only emits a stdout candidate via the non-blocking PostToolUse path; the Verifier (verifier.md key-behaviors, design.md:255-266) never reads wiring_checker output and never mentions WIRING items or the failed transition. No task wires the checker's unreachabl…

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a WIRING item is claimed proven, THE System SHALL require an integration test exercising the real execution path as the attached Evidence_Record.
ears_needing_work.how: Addressed by task 47 (WIRING integration-test evidence, previously untasked) — a WIRING item flips proven only with an integration test attached as Evidence_Record. This is the generic Evidence_Record gate; it does not involve wiring_checker.py at proof time.

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL represent architectural WIRING obligations and NFR items as first-class coverage items in feature_list.json, not as code comments or informal afterthoughts.
ears_needing_work.how: wiring_checker.py emits WIRING-typed item candidates (type 'WIRING' matches the CoverageItem type enum {functional,NFR,WIRING}, requirements.md:122 / design.md:333 / tasks.md:27). But because the candidate is stdout-only with no ingestion path into feature_list.json, the 'first-class coverage item i…

ears_needing_work.criterion: WIRING: A coverage-item type representing architectural connection obligations (routes, handlers, callbacks, jobs that must be reachable from a real execution path).
ears_needing_work.how: wiring_checker.py is the producer of WIRING-typed candidates; its detection targets (routes, handlers, callbacks, jobs / unreachable symbols) match the glossary scope. The pure-AST approach only finds defined-but-never-called functions; route/handler/callback decorator detection is delegated to the …

ears_needing_work.criterion: For any implementation unit with no requirement ID ref, and any requirement ID mapping to no verification artifact, the orphan-detection check shall fail.
ears_needing_work.how: DOES NOT bear on wiring_checker.py — Property 11 validates orphan_detector.py (a sibling Phase-1 tool, REQ-6.3), not wiring_checker.py. Listed here to record that the only Phase-1 dead-code Property is assigned to the OTHER tool; wiring_checker.py has no dedicated Property.
